mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth consistently correlates with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the correlation is statistically insignificant (p-value > 0.1) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates significantly above a low threshold, it signals potential central bank tightening or erosion of purchasing power, which typically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where the CPIAUCSL growth trigger is met.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales growth and a rising federal funds rate indicates an overheating economy where monetary policy is becoming restrictive to curb demand, which typically precedes a slowdown and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25% in the last month
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 shows positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods where both RSAFS growth and FEDFUNDS increase conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate during an economic downturn signals deepening recessionary pressures, leading to further declines in corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over the last 3 months
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 consistently generates positive 20-day returns when the UNRATE trigger is met during periods officially designated as recessionary.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects robust manufacturing activity and overall economic expansion, which translates into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth consistently correlates with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the correlation is statistically insignificant (p-value > 0.1) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts indicates weakening consumer confidence and investment in a key economic sector, signaling broader economic contraction and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 12-month moving average by more than 10%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where the HOUST decline trigger is met.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth is strong but the federal funds rate is kept low, it suggests an accommodative monetary policy supporting robust economic expansion, which boosts corporate valuations and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: If S&P 500 shows negative 5-day returns in more than 45% of periods where both GDPC1 growth and low FEDFUNDS conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: In an environment of high interest rates, any further acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals persistent inflationary pressures that could force even more aggressive monetary tightening, leading to significant negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where the CPIAUCSL trigger is met within a high FEDFUNDS regime.
